probe.reason: The same exact rational probe lies inside the M=11 and M=12 S phases and the M=13 gamma-minus phase.

moves: flip beta or gamma active-band sign
deactivate beta or gamma while one active P or Q contact leaves the reduced basis
excluded_moves: alpha sign flip or alpha deactivation
combined contact exchange and band sign flip

M13_global_extension.formal_result: M=13 has eight exact phases, seven simple finite transitions, unique integer-catalogue winner {2,3,4}, and a strictly increasing continuous first-anchor risk on [2,3).

formal_results: all 246 signatures in the declared one-pivot neighborhood were generated without manual filtering
M=11 and M=12 have the reference S signature as their unique strict local KKT optimum at the common exact probe
M=13 rejects S by a negative gamma-plus active multiplier
the gamma sign flip is the unique strict local optimum among the 89 M=13 neighbors
181 neighbors fail primal feasibility before any dual or reduced-cost test is needed
the first exact grammar break is therefore a pure completion-band sign selection, not a hidden contact exchange or numerical tie
the global first-boundary theorem extends to M=13 with eight exact phases
boundary: A73 is complete only for the explicitly declared one-pivot signature neighborhood at the exact probe s=33/256. It is not a complete enumeration of all LP bases or all feasible signatures across the full continuous alpha interval.
